content.problemStatement:
JJ has two binary strings AAA and BBB, both of length NNN. He can perform the following operations on AAA: \\
**1.** Pick any index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N) and flip AiA_iAi‚Äč (i.e. change 000 to 111 and 111 to 000) \\
**2.** Reverse AAA

JJ wants the number of inversions in AAA to be equal to the number of inversions in BBB by performing **at most** ‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ\lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ operations. Can you help him?

**Note:** The number of inversions in a binary string SSS is the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N and Si>SjS_i \gt S_jSi‚Äč>Sj‚Äč.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚ÄĒ the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the length of the binary strings AAA and BBB.
The second line of each test case contains a binary string AAA of length NNN containing 000s and 111s only.
The third line of each test case contains a binary string BBB of length NNN containing 000s and 111s only.


Output Format
For each test case, in the first line, output an integer MMM (0‚Č§M‚Č§‚ĆąN2‚ĆČ)(0 \le M \le \lceil \frac{N}{2} \rceil)(0‚Č§M‚Č§‚Ćą2N‚Äč‚ĆČ) - the number of operations. Output the operations performed in the following MMM lines. Output:



1 i\texttt{1 i}1 i: for denoting operation of type 111 on index iii.
2\texttt{2}2: for denoting operation of type 222.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: The following operation can be performed: 0101‚Üíreverse10100101 \xrightarrow{reverse} 10100101reverse‚Äč1010
inversions(1010)=inversions(1000)=3inversions(1010) = inversions(1000) = 3inversions(1010)=inversions(1000)=3
Test Case 2: No operations need to be performed since the number of inversions is already the same.
inversions(00)=inversions(11)=0inversions(00) = inversions(11) = 0inversions(00)=inversions(11)=0
Test Case 3: The following operations can be performed: 00001‚Üíreverse10000‚Üíflip¬†A41001000001 \xrightarrow{reverse} 10000 \xrightarrow{flip \ A_4} 1001000001reverse‚Äč10000flip¬†A4‚Äč‚Äč10010
inversions(10010)=inversions(11110)=4inversions(10010) = inversions(11110) = 4inversions(10010)=inversions(11110)=4
Test Case 4: The following operation can be performed: 00‚Üíflip¬†A11000 \xrightarrow{flip \ A_1} 1000flip¬†A1‚Äč‚Äč10
inversions(10)=inversions(10)=1inversions(10) = inversions(10) = 1inversions(10)=inversions(10)=1
